mechanism_narrative: The Fed's first rate hike off the zero lower bound (FEDFUNDS crossing above 0.25% after seven years near zero) introduces discount-rate repricing uncertainty and tightens financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as markets digest the regime shift.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 0.25% for the first time since 2008
evidence_refs: fact:FOMC meeting scheduled Dec 15-16 2015 widely expected to deliver liftoff
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following the liftoff trigger are positive or statistically indistinguishable from baseline (t-test p > 0.05) across the sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll gains (PAYEMS trending up) signal continued labor-market expansion and household income growth, which support consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 142,900 thousand as of latest reading with multi-month uptrend
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS trend and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) are jointly rising, the combined strength of consumer and housing demand signals a durable expansion phase that is not visible from either series alone, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter (60 trading days).
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 448,117 million and HOUST at 1,060 thousand units, both near cycle highs
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS-HOUST uptrend periods do not outperform periods where only one series rises, measured by average 60d forward return difference below 0.5 percentage points with p > 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: A break in industrial production (INDPRO) below its trailing 3-month moving average signals emerging manufacturing weakness that precedes broader earnings deceleration, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its trailing 3-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.2123, showing signs of industrial slowdown into late 2015
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns after INDPRO crosses below its 3-month moving average are not significantly negative (mean return >= 0 with p > 0.05) across the sample.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion with unemployment near estimates of full employment (UNRATE at or below 5.0%), a rising FEDFUNDS rate signals the Fed is responding to labor market tightness with tightening that risks choking growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter (60 trading days). The effect requires both conditions jointly, not either alone.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.0% coinciding with anticipated Fed liftoff
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during joint low-unemployment/rising-rate periods are not significantly more negative than during rising-rate periods with UNRATE above 5.5% (difference in means p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE falls to or below the 5.0% threshold widely viewed as consistent with full employment, markets price in accelerated wage and inflation pressure, prompting a hawkish repricing that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.0%, at the threshold commonly cited as full employment in 2015 FOMC commentary
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns immediately following UNRATE readings at or below 5.0% are positive or flat (mean >= 0) in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects broad-based economic expansion that supports corporate revenue and earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter (60 trading days).
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16,417.8 billion chained dollars, continuing multi-year expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing GDPC1 growth and forward 60d SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is rising while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, real short-term interest rates turn more negative, lowering the effective cost of capital and boosting equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days. This requires the joint condition of rising CPI and still-low FEDFUNDS.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 238.111 with FEDFUNDS still at 0.12% just before liftoff
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during joint rising-CPI/near-zero-FEDFUNDS periods are not significantly higher than unconditional average (t-test p > 0.05).
